currentTask.name: Harden provider runtime and Google model discovery
currentTask.goal: Fix Google model access issues, avoid hardcoded Gemini preview assumptions, support flexible OpenCode auth-profile switching, and document prompt/workflow lessons

environment.note: This workspace runs on Debian/WSL2. Global OpenCode runtime config is at /home/namikaz/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc.
environment.primaryTerminal: WSL Debian with native Linux tools

whatChanged: Removed small_model entries from the live OpenCode config after Google rejected that field in request payloads
Backed up the live OpenCode config before editing it
Synced 50 Google models from Google's OpenAI-compatible models endpoint into the live OpenCode config
Confirmed gemini-3.1-pro-preview is listed for the current Google key
Added google-models.sh to list and sync Google models without hardcoding preview names
Added opencode-auth-profile.sh to save and switch whole OpenCode auth profiles without printing secrets
Added docs/provider-runtime.md for Google model discovery, the small_model error, OpenCode Go account switching, memory expectations, and prompting best-practice mapping
Propagated the provider helper scripts to topic folders
verification: Direct Google OpenAI-compatible test returned 429 instead of the prior 400 malformed-payload error
residualRisk: The Google direct call hit 429, so payload shape is fixed but account quota/rate access still controls whether gemini-3.1-pro-preview can respond at a given moment.
immediateNextSteps: Restart OpenCode so it reads the updated config
Use bash scripts/google-models.sh whenever Google model availability is uncertain
Use bash scripts/opencode-auth-profile.sh save/use after connecting each OpenCode Go subscription
If Google still fails inside OpenCode, test with gemini-2.5-pro or gemini-3.1-pro-preview after quota recovers
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
